taskDescription: Drag the correct anatomical terms into the blanks to complete statements about the mouth, pharynx, and palate.
textField:
The parotid duct (Stensen's duct) opens into the vestibule of the mouth opposite the upper *second* molar tooth. The submandibular duct (*Wharton's* duct) opens on the sublingual papilla beside the frenulum of the tongue.

General sensation to the anterior two-thirds of the tongue is supplied by the *lingual* nerve (CN V3). Taste from the anterior two-thirds is carried by the *chorda tympani* nerve (CN VII). The *glossopharyngeal* nerve (CN IX) supplies both general sensation and taste to the posterior one-third.

All muscles of the tongue are supplied by the *hypoglossal* nerve (CN XII), except *palatoglossus* which is supplied by CN X. The primary protrusor of the tongue is the *genioglossus* muscle. Unilateral hypoglossal nerve palsy causes the tongue to deviate toward the *affected* side on protrusion.

The ring of lymphoid tissue at the pharyngeal entrance is called *Waldeyer's* lymphatic ring. Killian's dehiscence is the weak area in the *inferior constrictor* muscle where Zenker's diverticulum herniates. The motor supply to the pharyngeal constrictors is provided by the *vagus* nerve via the pharyngeal plexus.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the anatomy of the mouth, pharynx, and palate.
Good effort! Review the details you missed.
